title: Computer Use Action Execution
interaction_flow: ToolPlanning → execute_computer_actions(public_agent, actions: list[ToolRunComputerAction], hooks, context_wrapper, config)
execute_computer_actions: for each ToolRunComputerAction in actions (serial loop):
execute_computer_actions → on_safety_check(ComputerToolSafetyCheckData) [if pending_safety_checks exist on tool_call and tool has on_safety_check]
execute_computer_actions → ComputerAction.execute(agent, action, hooks, context_wrapper, config, acknowledged_safety_checks)
resolve_computer: if computer is ComputerProvider, call provider.create(run_context=context_wrapper) else return computer directly
ComputerAction → RunHooks.on_tool_start(context_wrapper, agent, computer_tool) [concurrent with agent_hooks]
_execute_action_and_capture: _iter_actions(tool_call) → list[action] (from tool_call.actions[] batch OR single tool_call.action)
_execute_action_and_capture: if last action was NOT screenshot → computer.screenshot() [always capture final state]
ComputerAction → RunHooks.on_tool_end(context_wrapper, agent, computer_tool, output) [concurrent with agent_hooks]

ComputerAction → execute_computer_actions: ToolCallOutputItem(agent, output=image_url, raw_item=ComputerCallOutput{type='computer_call_output', output={type='computer_screenshot', image_url=image_url}, acknowledged_safety_checks=...))
playwright_computer_detail: LocalPlaywrightComputer.__aenter__() / open(): async_playwright().start() → playwright.chromium.launch(headless=False, args=['--window-size=1024,768']) → browser.new_page() → page.set_viewport_size(1024x768) → page.goto('https://www.bing.com')
LocalPlaywrightComputer.click(x, y, button, keys): _hold_keys(keys) [keyboard.down each key] → page.mouse.click(x, y, button=playwright_button) → keyboard.up keys in reverse
LocalPlaywrightComputer.keypress(keys): _normalize_keys via CUA_KEY_TO_PLAYWRIGHT_KEY map → keyboard.down(key) for each → keyboard.up in reverse
LocalPlaywrightComputer.scroll(x, y, scroll_x, scroll_y, keys): _hold_keys → page.mouse.move(x, y) → page.evaluate('window.scrollBy(scroll_x, scroll_y)')
LocalPlaywrightComputer.drag(path, keys): _hold_keys → mouse.move(path[0]) → mouse.down() → mouse.move each subsequent point → mouse.up()
key_relationships: ComputerAction depends_on resolve_computer (from tool.py) to get ComputerLike instance
ComputerAction depends_on with_tool_function_span (from tool_execution.py) for tracing
LocalPlaywrightComputer extends AsyncComputer (abstract base in agents/computer.py)
ComputerTool contains computer: ComputerLike | ComputerCreate | ComputerProvider
ComputerProvider has create: ComputerCreate + dispose: ComputerDispose (per-run lifecycle)
ToolRunComputerAction is dataclass with tool_call: ResponseComputerToolCall + computer_tool: ComputerTool
ComputerAction._execute_action_and_capture handles both sync Computer and AsyncComputer (via inspect.isawaitable)
modifier keys passed via _filter_supported_kwargs — drops keys= if driver doesn't support it (with warning)
important_notes: Batched actions: GA tool sends actions[] array; legacy sends single action field — both supported via _iter_actions()
Screenshot optimization: if last action in batch is screenshot, reuse its result instead of capturing twice
Safety checks: pending_safety_checks on tool_call → on_safety_check callback → acknowledged_safety_checks included in ComputerCallOutput
Tracing: span name is 'computer' (TRACE_TOOL_NAME), input/output redacted when trace_include_sensitive_data=False
Two usage modes: singleton (shared AsyncComputer) vs per-request (ComputerProvider with create/dispose hooks)
resolve_computer uses WeakKeyDictionary to cache per run_context to avoid re-creating
ComputerAction._filter_supported_kwargs: inspects method signature, drops unsupported kwargs (e.g. 'keys') with logger.warning for legacy drivers
Output format: image_url = 'data:image/png;base64,{base64_string}' — wrapped in ComputerCallOutput raw_item
